prompts.title: 3-Second Hook Bank for Indian Reels
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior Instagram Reels strategist who has scaled Indian creator pages from 0 to 500K. I create [NICHE: lifestyle/fashion/fitness/food/comedy/motivation/education] content in [LANGUAGE: Hindi/Hinglish/Tamil/Kannada/Bengali/Marathi/Punjabi]. Build me a bank of 30 distinct 3-second opening hooks engineered for the Indian Reels algorithm in 2026. For each hook give: (1) the exact spoken line in my language with English translation, (2) the on-screen text overlay (max 6 words, Devanagari/regional script if relevant), (3) the visual action happening on camera in second 0-3, (4) the emotional driver (curiosity gap, FOMO, contrarian, relatable pain, status, controversy, transformation tease), (5) the niche it suits best, and (6) a sample first comment to seed engagement. Cover hook archetypes proven on Indian Reels: 'Sach batau?' confession, 'Mummy ne mana kiya tha but...', 'Yeh galti mat karna', '90% Indians don't know this', price-reveal shock, before-photo reveal, 'Bina paise wala hack', 'Maine try kiya 30 din', 'Delhi vs Mumbai vs Bangalore' comparison, regional pride hooks. Avoid generic Western templates like 'POV you woke up'. End with a 5-question scorecard I can run any new hook through (Stop scroll? Curiosity? Promise payoff? Native to my audience? Loop-friendly?) before I shoot. Also flag 5 hook formats that are now overused in India in 2026 and feel cringe to Gen Z viewers.

prompts.title: Scroll-Stopper Visual Patterns by Niche
prompts.prompt: You are a Reels visual director who studies thumbnail-frame psychology. My niche is [NICHE] and my typical viewer is an Indian [AGE BAND] who scrolls Reels during commute / lunch break / 11pm bed-scroll. Design 12 scroll-stopper visual patterns specific to my niche, where the FIRST FRAME alone makes a thumb pause. For fashion: outfit-on-hanger-to-body transition, price-tag reveal, saree-drape time-lapse start frame. For fitness: belly photo with measuring tape overlay, dumbbell weight close-up with shock face. For food: sizzle pan with steam, hand-pour cheese pull, thali overhead with empty centre plate. For comedy: freeze-frame with red arrow, character-in-costume awkward pose. For motivation: text-only black frame with bold Hindi quote, 'kal raat 2 baje' timestamp overlay. For education: handwritten formula with mistake circled, calculator with viral number. Specify for each: framing (vertical 9:16 safe zone with IG UI overlap zones marked), lighting setup achievable with one ring light or window, colour contrast that survives compression, the prop list under 500 rupees, and the exact text overlay style (font, size, drop-shadow, regional script support). Finish with a weekly shoot plan that batches 7 hook visuals in one 90-minute session.

prompts.title: Looping Audio Hook Engineering
prompts.prompt: Act as a Reels audio editor specialising in seamless loops that trick the algorithm into counting double watch-time. I want to design Reels where the last frame audio bleeds invisibly into the first frame. Walk me through: (1) a framework to identify which trending Indian sounds (Bollywood drops, Punjabi beats, regional folk hooks, viral dialogues) have a natural loop point versus which die at the end, (2) how to cut my voice-over so the closing word sets up the opening word visually, (3) the 'visual rhyme' technique where a hand gesture, eye-blink or product-pickup at second 14 mirrors second 0, (4) a 6-step Reels-Editor or CapCut workflow to test the loop on mute and with sound, (5) 5 hook-loop archetypes that work for Indian audiences (the unanswered question, the count-up trick, the mid-action freeze, the false ending, the rhetorical 'samjhe?'). Give me 10 example loop scripts of 12-22 seconds each across niches relevant to me. Also explain the difference in retention impact between a smooth loop and a 'reset cut' for Indian viewers who are still trained on TV ad pacing, and how to A/B test two loop variants of the same reel via the close-friends story trick before public posting.

prompts.title: POV Script Generator for Indian Contexts
prompts.prompt: You are a comedy and storytelling writer who has scripted POV reels for top Indian creators in lifestyle, relationships and workplace humour. Generate 20 POV reel scripts grounded in everyday Indian life that my [NICHE] audience will recognise instantly. Cover situations like: 'POV: tu Bangalore traffic mein interview ke liye late hai', 'POV: shaadi mein WhatsApp uncle ne tujhe pakda', 'POV: tu Zomato delivery ka order kholti hai aur free dessert nikalta hai', 'POV: tujhe Diwali bonus mil gaya', 'POV: Bengaluru mein 1BHK rent dekh ke', 'POV: Karwa Chauth pe husband bhool gaya', 'POV: Onam sadya mein last banana chip', 'POV: Pongal pe ammama ka call'. For each script provide: a one-line setup, three escalating beats, a punchline or twist that lands in under 18 seconds, the ideal Hindi/regional sound to layer underneath, the on-screen text choreography, and a single CTA designed to drive comments ('Tag woh dost', 'Yeh kis state mein hota hai?'). Add a section on cultural sensitivity: which religious, caste, regional or political angles to AVOID, and how to do gentle observational comedy without punching down. Close with a 5-prompt template I can reuse weekly to spin new POVs from headlines and personal life.

prompts.title: Day-in-Life Vlog Reel Structure
prompts.prompt: Act as a creator coach who helped lifestyle creators in Tier-2 Indian cities build aspirational-yet-relatable day-in-life reels that hit 1M+ views. Design me a repeatable 30-60 second day-in-life reel template optimised for Reels (not YouTube Shorts, not TikTok), tuned for an Indian audience that wants either aspiration (Bandra apartment morning routine) or relatability (PG-girl-in-Pune budget day). Provide: (1) a 9-shot storyboard with timestamps (alarm, chai/coffee, mirror outfit, commute, work scene, lunch, golden-hour break, evening hustle, wind-down), (2) the 'hero shot' that has to be cinematic and how to capture it on a basic phone with a 200-rupee tripod, (3) the on-screen-text rhythm that names each segment in Hinglish, (4) the trending-audio strategy for this format in 2026 (slow Bollywood acoustic vs lo-fi vs Punjabi sad), (5) the caption template that converts viewers to followers ('Day 47 of building my brand from a Jaipur PG'), (6) the disclosure rule when one of the products on screen is a paid placement under ASCI 2024 guidelines. Add a list of 10 niche variants: corporate-girl-Gurgaon, freelancer-Goa, MBBS-student-Manipal, food-vendor-Kolkata, gym-trainer-Indore, Bharatanatyam-dancer-Chennai, NRI-visiting-home, etc.

prompts.title: Before/After Transformation Reel Blueprint
prompts.prompt: You are a Reels editor who has crafted before/after reels in fitness, fashion, makeup, home-decor and skill-acquisition niches that consistently break 5M views in India. Build me a transformation reel system. Cover: (1) the psychology of the Indian viewer who wants 'sasta, sundar, tikau' transformation proof, (2) reveal mechanics — instant cut, swipe-wipe, zoom-pull, hand-cover-face reveal, mirror-spin, jacket-flick — with examples for my niche [NICHE], (3) the 'three-act tease' where I show the before, hint at the method for 8 seconds, then reveal, designed to maximise rewatch loops, (4) honest disclosure: how to show realistic timelines ('6 mahine ka result, edited in 15 sec') so I stay ASCI-compliant and avoid the misleading-claims complaints that hit fitness creators in 2024-25, (5) audio choices that build tension then drop on reveal (Bollywood orchestral hits, Punjabi dhol drops, KGF-style score), (6) a caption that drives DM enquiries for my paid program or affiliate, (7) carousel companion of 8 slides expanding the method. Give me 5 fully scripted before/after reels across saree-styling, weight-loss, skin-glow, room-makeover-under-5k, English-fluency. Each script must include exact dialogue, shot list, music cue points, and a follow-up reel idea to milk the same transformation.

prompts.title: Listicle Reel Format that Holds Watch Time
prompts.prompt: Act as a Reels writer who specialises in listicle formats (5 things, 3 hacks, 7 mistakes) that score 90%+ average watch time on Indian audiences. Design me a listicle reel framework. Output: (1) the optimal item count for a 30-45 second reel (3, 5 or 7) with reasoning based on attention curves, (2) the 'reverse-rank reveal' where I count down from item 5 to item 1 with item 1 being so good viewers screenshot, (3) the timestamp progress bar / numbered chapter overlay technique that keeps viewers because they want to see all items, (4) a 'last item is a paid plug' structure compliant with ASCI's #ad / #sponsored disclosure placed in the first three lines of caption AND on-screen, (5) niche-specific listicle banks: 5 Bandra cafes under 500, 7 sarees under 2000 from Surat, 3 protein meals under 100 rupees, 5 books that changed my CAT prep, 7 desi grandmother skincare hacks, 5 Bengaluru weekend trips under 3000. Provide 15 ready-to-shoot listicle scripts with hook, transitions between items, on-screen text per item, and an end-card CTA that drives 'save' (the strongest growth signal in 2026). Include a 'don't do this' list: items that look like clickbait but underdeliver, list lengths that kill retention, generic hashtags that throttle reach.

prompts.title: Try-vs-Buy Reel for Affiliate Conversion
prompts.prompt: You are an affiliate-marketing-aware Reels strategist who has driven crores in GMV through Amazon Influencer, Myntra Studio, Meesho Glam and Flipkart VIP for Indian creators. Build me a try-vs-buy reel system that converts views to clicks. Components: (1) the format split — 'try-on haul', 'taste test', 'unboxing reveal', 'side-by-side comparison with cheaper dupe', 'price reveal at end' — and which works for my niche [NICHE], (2) the camera setup for clean product hero shots on a phone using diffused window light and a white sheet, (3) the rating rubric I verbalise on camera (fit, fabric, value-for-money, dupe-worth, 5-star scale) so viewers trust me, (4) the disclosure stack: 'Paid partnership with…' label, #ad in caption, spoken disclosure within first 5 seconds — exactly per ASCI 2024 guidelines, (5) the link-in-bio funnel using LinkTree or Beacons or my own page on Stan/Kajabi to track UTM, (6) caption template: hook + verdict + price + 'link in bio under Reel #'. Provide 10 try-vs-buy scripts across Myntra fashion haul, Nykaa skincare, Amazon kitchen gadgets, Flipkart Diwali decor, Meesho saree dupes. End with a math model showing how a creator with 50K followers earning 4-8% commission can hit 25K-60K rupees monthly affiliate income from 8 reels per month.

prompts.title: Get-Ready-With-Me Reel Choreography
prompts.prompt: Act as a beauty and lifestyle Reels director who has built GRWM formats for Indian weddings, festivals, college fests and date nights. Choreograph me a 30-second GRWM reel template. Provide: (1) the chronological 8-shot sequence — wet hair towel, base prep, eye look, lip, hair, outfit reveal, accessories, final twirl, (2) the 'voice-over storytelling' layer where I narrate where I'm going and the personal context ('aaj sasural mein pehli Karwa Chauth') because Indian viewers want story not just visuals, (3) the trending audio choices for 2026 that pace each section perfectly (slow intro Bollywood, beat-drop on outfit reveal), (4) the product-name on-screen-text discipline (every product gets a 1-second tag with brand name and price, fully sponsorable), (5) the festival GRWM calendar — Karwa Chauth, Navratri 9 looks, Diwali, Christmas, Eid, Pongal kolam GRWM, Onam half-saree, Bihu mekhela chador, Punjabi mehendi function, Bengali shubho bijoya — with one signature reel idea per festival, (6) the caption + hashtag stack for each festival to ride the search wave. Add a brand-deal pitch template: 'I'm doing 4 Karwa Chauth GRWMs in October, would love to integrate your kumkum / lehenga / bangles, here are my last 3 GRWM analytics screenshots'.

prompts.title: Trending Audio Finder Framework for India
prompts.prompt: You are a Reels sound strategist who builds custom audio-discovery systems. The Indian Reels trending charts are flooded with Bollywood drops, regional language hits, dialogue clips and creator-original sounds. Design me a daily 15-minute audio-finding workflow that surfaces sounds with 5K-50K reels (the sweet spot — big enough to be trending, small enough to not be saturated). Cover: (1) the in-app discovery hacks — 'used in X reels' counter, the audio page reels-grid scan, the 'For You' audio-bookmark behaviour, the upward-arrow trending icon, (2) the external sources — Reelsaudio.in, GrowthSchool sound trackers, Notion templates from top creators, Telegram channels for Bollywood drops, regional language Insta pages (e.g. Tamil dialogue compilations), (3) a saved-folder system inside Instagram tagged by mood — chill, hype, sad, comedy, motivational, festive, regional — so I have 20 audios ready to deploy any day, (4) the 24-72 hour 'jump-on' window after which a sound becomes a graveyard, (5) how to spot a sound that's about to break (creators with 2K followers using it before the celebrities do), (6) the original-audio play: when to record my own voice/song so it can become trending and I get notified every time someone uses it. Provide a 7-day audio scouting checklist.

prompts.title: Original Audio vs Trend Ratio Strategy
prompts.prompt: Act as a creator-economy analyst who has audited the audio strategy of 100+ Indian creators between 50K and 5M followers. Build me a personalised audio-mix policy. Inputs I'll give you: my niche [NICHE], my current follower count [COUNT], my goals (reach vs monetisation vs personal-brand authority). Output should include: (1) the recommended ratio — e.g. 60% trending audio, 30% original voice-over, 10% original music/jingle — and why this differs for a 10K motivational creator vs a 200K fashion creator vs a 1M comedy creator, (2) the trade-off table: trending sounds get reach but lower brand-deal RPM because they can't be reused; original sounds get lower initial reach but compound (every cover use is a notification + free promo), (3) when a Bollywood-licensed track becomes legally unsafe for a paid brand reel and Meta strips audio (Karwa Chauth 2025 takedown wave), (4) how to record a signature jingle / catchphrase / 5-second sound logo that becomes my creator audio fingerprint, (5) how regional creators (Kannada, Punjabi, Bengali, Marathi) should weight regional-sound % vs pan-India trending sounds for both reach and community ownership, (6) a 30-day calendar mapping audio type to reel slot. End with three example creators (anonymised) showing how shifting from 90% trending to 60-30-10 grew their brand-deal income.

prompts.title: Voice-Over vs Lip-Sync Decision Tree
prompts.prompt: You are a Reels content director who coaches Indian creators on whether each idea should be lip-synced to a trending sound, voice-overed in their own language, or shot as silent visual with text overlay. Build me a decision tree. The tree must consider: my niche [NICHE], the goal of the reel (reach, save, comment, DM, link-click), my comfort with on-camera dialogue, my language strength (English vs Hindi vs Hinglish vs regional), and the audio's licensing for brand use. For each output branch (lip-sync / voice-over / silent-with-text / hybrid) provide: (1) when it works best with examples in Indian context, (2) the production checklist including microphone choice (collar mic under 1500 rupees, BOYA M1 vs MAONO), recording environment (cupboard treatment for echo), software (CapCut, InShot, VN), (3) the retention curve I should expect — voice-overs hold longer for educational, lip-sync drives shares for comedy, silent-text wins saves for hacks, (4) accent and dialect notes for regional creators — when Tanglish, Manglish or Bambaiya Hindi is an asset vs liability, (5) the brand-deal premium — voice-over and original audio reels typically command 1.5-2x rates because the brand can re-use without licensing risk. Include 10 sample reel ideas with the decision-tree verdict applied.

prompts.title: 3-Cut Formula for Reels
prompts.prompt: Act as a Reels editor who teaches the 3-cut formula proven on Indian creator pages: hook-shot, content-shot, payoff-shot, with everything else trimmed. Walk me through: (1) the philosophy — Indian viewers raised on 30-second TV ads have built tolerance for fast cuts, but the algorithm rewards completion, so simpler-than-Western edits actually win, (2) for my niche [NICHE], how to pre-plan only 3 shots so I shoot in 10 minutes instead of 60, (3) cut-point craftsmanship: cut on motion (hand sweep), cut on beat (audio drop), cut on word (closing syllable), cut on direction-change (head turn left to right), (4) the 'no transition' transition — straight cut with matching framing — vs the overused CapCut zoom transition that now feels 2022, (5) how to add subtle on-screen-text that survives compression and is readable on a 5-inch screen, (6) the Reels editor in-app vs CapCut workflow with timestamps. Provide 5 worked examples: a fashion outfit 3-cut, a fitness exercise 3-cut, a recipe 3-cut, a motivational quote 3-cut, an education tip 3-cut. Include a 'kill list' of overused effects (slow-mo on every shot, rainbow flares, AI face-warp) that brand managers in 2026 see as low-quality and refuse to pay premium for.

prompts.title: Transition Bank Beyond CapCut Defaults
prompts.prompt: You are a Reels VFX coach. CapCut, VN and the in-app Reels editor offer the same default transitions every creator is using, which makes content feel templated. Give me a transition bank of 25 shot-to-shot moves I can execute on a phone in under 5 minutes per cut, that look unique on the Indian Reels feed in 2026. For each transition include: (1) name, (2) the in-camera technique (hand wipe, jacket flick, towel toss, dupatta swirl, plate cover, lehenga twirl, door slam, mirror spin, dosa flip, gym belt fasten), (3) the post-edit cut on beat, (4) the niches it suits, (5) the difficulty 1-5, (6) example reels of Indian creators who use it well (describe pattern, do not name accounts). Cover transitions that match Indian visual idioms — diya light-up, rangoli reveal, sindoor close-up, henna-hand-to-bridal, biryani lid-lift steam reveal, lassi pour, gym mirror to bicep, scooter helmet flip, autorickshaw door, train-window blur, festival-light-string. Provide a 'transition-of-the-week' calendar I can practice over 12 weeks to build muscle memory. Add a section on when NOT to use a transition: serious motivational reel, condolence content, food where the dish needs to breathe, educational explainers.

prompts.title: B-Roll Shot List for Indian Creators
prompts.prompt: Act as a director of photography for Reels. I'm a [NICHE] creator with a phone, one ring light, and 90 minutes once a week to shoot B-roll I can drop into reels for an entire month. Design me a 50-shot B-roll list with shot specs for each: framing (close-up, medium, wide, top-down, POV-handheld), camera move (static, slow push, slow pull, tilt-up, lock-down), duration (2, 3, 5 seconds), prop, location, and which type of reel each shot supports (hook, transition, payoff, mood-bridge). Tailor the list to Indian backgrounds my audience will recognise — chai stall steam, scooter mirror, train window, autorickshaw POV, sabzi mandi colours, Marine Drive at golden hour, terrace-with-water-tank, jaali shadows, Bandstand, Cubbon Park, Necklace Road Hyderabad, Marina Beach Chennai, Park Street Kolkata, Connaught Place, Hauz Khas. Add 10 'evergreen aesthetic' shots that look premium even when dated — hand-pouring chai, journaling pen close-up, candle flame, rain on window, book pages turning, incense smoke, bangle-stack twist, mehendi hand. Output a one-page shoot plan I can take to my chosen location with prop checklist, time block per shot, and a rule for when to delete a shot vs save it for archive.

prompts.title: Location Scout Brief for Reels
prompts.prompt: You are a Mumbai/Bangalore/Delhi-based content production scout. I'm a creator in [CITY] with a budget of [BUDGET in rupees per shoot day]. Build me a location scout brief covering 12 distinct locations within 30 km of my city that elevate my reels without looking like every other creator's spot. For each location include: (1) name and access notes, (2) entry fee or permission required, (3) best time slot for natural light and low crowd, (4) the 'signature shot' the location offers, (5) hidden corners that other creators have missed, (6) parking and equipment-haul logistics, (7) safety notes for a solo female creator, (8) the kind of niche reels it suits — fashion try-on, food review, yoga, motivational walking shot, vlog intro. Cover beyond-obvious places: heritage stepwells, vintage cafes, terrace gardens, art-district lanes, factory-conversion spaces, niche bookstores, lesser-known beach pockets, metro-station architecture, government-building exteriors that are public-photography-allowed. Add a polite Hindi/regional script I can use to ask local shopkeepers for permission to film for 10 minutes. End with a quarterly rotation calendar so I never repeat the same location more than once a quarter, keeping my feed visually fresh and my audience guessing where I'll show up next.

prompts.title: Caption Hooks that Drive Saves and Shares
prompts.prompt: Act as a copywriter who has written captions for top Indian Reels creators that hit save:like ratios above 1:5 — the strongest growth signal in 2026. Build me a caption framework. Output: (1) the structure — hook line (visible above the 'more' fold), context, value, CTA, hashtags — with character counts that survive Reels caption truncation, (2) 30 hook lines for my niche [NICHE] in Hinglish that earn the tap-to-expand, e.g. 'Yeh save kar le, baad mein search nahi karna padega', 'Mummy ko mat dikhana lekin…', '5 saal ki maine ki galti…', (3) the value-stack body — bullet points with emoji markers that scan in 3 seconds, (4) the CTA matrix mapping save / share / comment / follow / DM / link-click to one specific phrasing each ('Save karo, kal try karna', 'Tag woh dost jo Bandra mein rehta hai', 'Comment 'YES' for the link'), (5) a hashtag-and-mention sub-block at the end so the body looks clean, (6) a special caption pattern for paid reels that places #ad / 'Paid partnership with @brand' in the first line per ASCI 2024 rules. Give me 10 worked caption examples for fashion, fitness, food, motivation, education, regional, comedy, lifestyle, NRI-targeting and giveaway reels.

prompts.title: Story-CTA Caption Recipes
prompts.prompt: You are a community-building strategist for Indian Reels creators. The most undervalued growth lever is the caption that turns a passive viewer into a story-watcher and DM-replier. Design 15 'story-CTA caption' recipes that explicitly route reel viewers to a specific story I'll have live for 24 hours. Each recipe should include: (1) the reel topic it pairs with, (2) the caption sentence that nudges the viewer ('Full prep behind this look in my story right now — link sticker bhi hai'), (3) the matching story content sequence (3-7 frames) — teaser, depth, social proof, CTA, link sticker, poll, DM-prompt, (4) the conversion mechanic — link sticker to affiliate page, DM auto-response keyword, close-friends invite for paid community, (5) Indian-context behaviour notes — Indian viewers are heavy story-tappers but slow link-clickers, so the link sticker needs a money-on-the-table hook ('Discount code valid till 11:59 tonight'), (6) a metric to track (story:reel view ratio target 8-15%, link-sticker click-through 1-3%). Cover use cases: launching my own product, hyping a brand collab, recruiting beta-testers for a paid course, asking for content suggestions, polling my audience on a niche-pivot decision, soft-pitching my next live, opening DMs for 1-1 consults at 999 rupees a slot.

prompts.title: Niche-Specific Caption Banks
prompts.prompt: Act as a copywriting director. Build me a 100-line caption bank specifically for my niche [NICHE: pick one — fashion / fitness / food / comedy / motivation / education / lifestyle / regional]. Lines should be in [LANGUAGE: Hindi / Hinglish / Tamil / Kannada / Bengali / Marathi / Punjabi]. Organise into 10 buckets of 10 captions each: (1) confessional ('Sach mein, yeh outfit pehen ke pehli baar confidence aaya'), (2) controversial ('Saree pe heels? Mat suno aunties ko'), (3) empathy ('Pata hai, gym jaane ki himmat hi nahi hoti'), (4) curiosity-gap ('Iska secret ingredient guess karo'), (5) value-bomb ('5 minute mein puri Bollywood smoky eye'), (6) social-proof ('5000 logon ne try kiya, 4500 ko yeh galti hui'), (7) story-drop ('Jab maine pehli baar Mumbai aayi thi…'), (8) festival-tied ('Karwa Chauth ke liye yeh look bachakar rakho'), (9) community-call ('Bengaluru creators, comment your fav cafe'), (10) brand-slot ('Paid partnership with @brand — yeh wala favourite ban gaya'). Each line must be ready to copy-paste, under 220 characters so it fits above the fold, and pass ASCI disclosure rules where applicable. Add usage guidance: rotate buckets so feed never feels repetitive, never use confessional and controversial back-to-back, festival captions go up 7 days before the date for the search-wave.

prompts.title: Comment-Bait Strategy that Doesn't Feel Cheap
prompts.prompt: You are a Reels growth coach who has tested comment-bait techniques across thousands of Indian creator reels and knows which feel manipulative (and get downranked) vs which feel native and boost reach 3-5x. Design me a comment-bait playbook. Output: (1) the algorithmic logic in 2026 — comments are weighted higher than likes, comment-replies even more, threaded conversations highest, so the goal is to spark dialogue not just one-word replies, (2) 20 prompt patterns that work on Indian audiences — 'pick A, B or C', 'guess the city', 'finish this line', 'rate out of 10', 'spell my name in your language', 'tag a friend who would do this', 'unpopular opinion', 'roast my outfit', 'agree or disagree', 'what's your version of this story', (3) the 'first comment' I should post myself within 60 seconds of going live to seed the conversation thread, (4) the reply discipline — answer the first 30 comments within 90 minutes with personalised replies (not heart emoji) to multiply weight, (5) the dark-pattern list to avoid — fake controversy, divisive religion/politics bait, misleading 'comment 'YES' for free product' that you don't deliver, because these get ASCI complaints and shadow-bans. Provide 10 niche-specific comment-bait reels I can shoot this week.

prompts.title: Per-Niche Hashtag Mix for India
prompts.prompt: Act as a hashtag strategist who has tested 1000+ tag combinations across Indian Reels niches. Build me a hashtag mix for my niche [NICHE] that uses the 2026 Instagram model where tags are still indexed but capped at meaningful 5-8 (over-tagging now penalised). Output: (1) a 30-tag pool divided into 4 buckets — 5 mega tags (1M+ posts, e.g. #reelsindia #explorepage), 10 niche tags (100K-1M, e.g. #indianfashionblogger #desifoodie), 10 micro tags (10K-100K, e.g. #bandrastyle #bangalorefoodguide), 5 branded/community tags (mine + 2-3 collab partners), (2) the rotation rule — never use the exact same combination twice in a week, pick 5 from the pool per reel, always include 1 city/region tag for local reach, (3) the regional language tag set if I'm a Kannada/Bengali/Tamil/Marathi/Punjabi creator (#kannadareels, #banglareels, #marathireels, #punjabireels, #tamilreels) and how using regional tags can earn 30-50% more native-language reach, (4) the festival overlay tags activated 14-7 days pre-festival (#karwachauth2026, #onam, #pongal, #christmasreels, #eidcollection, #bihu), (5) the 5 tags currently flagged or saturated I should drop from my old templates. Add a monthly hashtag audit checklist.

prompts.title: Hashtag Rotation and Audit System
prompts.prompt: You are a data-driven creator coach. I want a monthly hashtag audit and rotation system that I can run in 30 minutes the first weekend of every month. Build it. Cover: (1) the data inputs from Instagram Insights — top 9 hashtags driving reach in last 30 days, average reach per hashtag, conversion to follow, (2) the Notion/Sheets template with columns (tag, post-volume bucket, my reach contribution, last 30-day trend up/down, retire-or-keep), (3) the swap rule — replace the bottom 3 performers each month with 3 new candidates from competitor scraping or recent trending tags, (4) how to spot a tag that's been flagged or de-indexed (impressions cliff, no new reels showing, search returns 'recent' but not 'top'), (5) the seasonal swap calendar — IPL season tags, exam-results tags, monsoon tags, wedding-season tags, festival-tags, year-end tags, (6) how regional creators should rebalance regional vs pan-India tags as their following grows from city-fame to state-fame to national, (7) the 'branded community tag' tracker — am I getting 5+ new uses of my own tag per week from followers, indicating organic community formation. Provide a sample audit for a fitness creator at 80K followers showing real swap decisions and projected reach lift.

prompts.title: Branded Hashtag Launch Campaign
prompts.prompt: Act as a community-marketing strategist who has launched branded hashtags for Indian Reels creators that crossed 10K user-generated uses within 90 days. Design me a branded hashtag launch for my creator brand [BRAND/HANDLE] in niche [NICHE]. Output: (1) hashtag-name brainstorm rules — must be 2-3 words, easy to type in English script, no special characters, available across Insta/YT/X, ideally one searchable Hinglish phrase ('#ChaiPeKahaani', '#GymWaliBai', '#SareeSquad', '#ReelsKaRaja'), (2) the launch reel — origin story of the tag in 30 seconds, the 'rules' of the community, what happens to people who post with it (regrant on my page, monthly best-of, prize), (3) the seeding plan — I tag 10 friend-creators on day 1 to use it before public launch, building a baseline of 30-50 quality posts so newcomers see an active community not a ghost tag, (4) the weekly ritual — every Sunday I do a 'Top 5 of the week with #BrandedTag' reel, building the FOMO loop, (5) the brand-deal monetisation — once tag has 5K+ uses, I sell it to a brand as a co-creation property ('30-day #BrandedTag challenge sponsored by @brand', rate card 3-7 lakhs depending on reach), (6) ASCI-disclosure layer when sponsored. Add a 90-day milestone tracker.

prompts.title: 10-Slide Carousel as Reel-Companion
prompts.prompt: You are a carousel design strategist. Reels drive reach, carousels drive saves and depth — together they compound. Design me a system to publish a 10-slide carousel that companions every flagship reel I post in [NICHE]. Output: (1) the carousel structure — slide 1 hook (mirrors reel hook), slides 2-3 context, slides 4-8 the meat (steps, examples, lists), slide 9 case study or proof, slide 10 CTA + my handle for share-screenshots, (2) the design system — vertical 4:5, brand-colour-palette of 3 colours, one signature font in Devanagari or regional script if relevant, max 30 words per slide, all built in Canva from one master template I can spin in 20 minutes, (3) the 'reel-to-carousel' content recycle — take the 30-second reel script and expand each beat into a slide, so I never start from scratch, (4) the caption — the carousel caption can be longer (essay-style) since carousels invite long captions, with a tap-to-expand hook, (5) the cross-post timing — carousel goes up 24-48 hours after the reel hits its peak, capturing the 'I want more from this creator' surge, (6) the analytics target — saves should be 2-4x the reel's saves, follows from carousel are 1.5-2x. Provide 5 worked reel-plus-carousel pairings for my niche with full slide outlines.

prompts.title: Slide-Deck-Style Reel for Educators
prompts.prompt: Act as an educational content director who has built slide-deck-style reels for Indian creators in finance, English-fluency, UPSC prep, productivity, mental health, parenting, and sex-ed-for-women — formats that hit 90%+ completion rates. Build me a slide-deck reel template. Components: (1) the 8-slide-in-30-seconds rhythm — slide 1 hook (3 sec), slides 2-7 lessons (3-4 sec each), slide 8 CTA (3 sec), (2) the visual design — clean background colour, one bold headline per slide, one supporting image or icon, my face in a small corner cutout for trust, (3) the voice-over scripted in Hindi/Hinglish that matches the on-screen text but adds spoken context (the on-screen is the headline, the voice gives the example), (4) the 'one big takeaway' rule — every reel must leave the viewer with one phrase they could repeat to a friend, (5) the proof-stack — credentials, source citations on slides where I claim a stat, link to source in caption to satisfy fact-checking and avoid misinformation flags, (6) the lead-magnet hook — 'Comment WORKBOOK and I'll DM you the free PDF', driving DM volume which Meta now weighs heavily. Provide 10 reel topics across personal-finance-for-women, English-grammar-mistakes, UPSC-mains-tips, parenting-toddlers-Indian-context, breath-work-for-anxiety.

prompts.title: Brand-Deal Rate Card by Follower Count
prompts.prompt: You are a creator-economy economist with access to anonymised Indian rate-card data from talent agencies (Monk-E, OpraahFx, BigBang.Social, Collective Artists, OML, Hashfame). Build me a transparent rate card for Reels brand deals based on follower count and engagement, accurate for the Indian market in 2026. Output a table covering: (1) follower bands — 5K-25K (nano), 25K-100K (micro), 100K-500K (mid), 500K-1M (macro), 1M+ (mega), (2) base rate per single Reel (no story, no carousel) in INR for each band, (3) bundle rate for 1 Reel + 3 stories + 1 static post, (4) usage rights premium — organic only vs 30-day boost vs 90-day perpetual, (5) exclusivity premium — 30/60/90 day category exclusivity uplift %, (6) niche multipliers — finance and tech command 1.5x, lifestyle and fashion are baseline, regional creators command 0.7-0.9x but with rising trend, education and parenting 1.2x, (7) the engagement-rate adjustment — sub-2% ER takes a 30% haircut, 5%+ ER earns 25% premium, (8) deliverable extras — BTS reel, raw footage, brand reel re-grant. Add a 'red flag' list — brand requests that always need extra fees (script approval rounds, dance choreography, on-set whole day, location travel). End with a negotiation script in Hinglish for politely rejecting lowball offers.

prompts.title: Brand Pitch DM Template
prompts.prompt: Act as a creator-business manager who has secured brand deals worth 50L+ for Indian creators across DTC, FMCG, fashion, finance and edtech. Write me a brand pitch DM template that I can personalise and send 10 times a week. Cover: (1) the cold DM structure — line 1 personal observation about the brand (proves I researched), line 2 my creator profile in one sentence with key metric, line 3 why my audience overlaps theirs, line 4 a specific reel concept tailored to a current campaign of theirs, line 5 the ask — 30-min call or media kit share, (2) the warm DM via mutual — leveraging an existing creator-friend's intro, (3) the LinkedIn parallel — finding the brand-marketing-manager and sending a follow-up email 24 hours after Insta DM with my media kit attached as PDF, (4) the media kit one-pager — handle, niche, follower count, demographics (age, gender, top 5 cities, India vs NRI %), 90-day reach, top 3 reels with metrics, past brand collabs, rate card, contact, (5) the follow-up cadence — day 0 DM, day 4 nudge, day 10 final follow-up with a different angle, then move on, (6) Indian etiquette notes — address as 'Hi [name]' not 'Dear sir/ma'am', avoid 'kindly', keep it crisp 80-120 words. Provide 5 worked DMs to a fashion DTC, a fintech app, a regional-foods FMCG, an edtech, and a tier-2 jewellery brand.

prompts.title: Scope of Work Document for Brand Reels
prompts.prompt: You are a creator-side legal and operations consultant. Most Indian creator-brand disputes happen because the SoW was a WhatsApp text instead of a document. Build me a one-page Scope of Work template I can send to every brand before payment. Cover sections: (1) parties — my legal name / sole-proprietor / LLP / Pvt Ltd, brand legal entity and GSTIN, (2) deliverables — exact count (e.g. 1 Reel 30-45 sec, 3 stories with link sticker, 1 static post, 1 carousel), platform (Instagram only or cross-post to YT Shorts), (3) timelines — script approval window 48 hours, shoot date, edit submission, brand revision rounds capped at 2, go-live date, (4) usage rights — organic only vs paid boost duration vs whitelisting via Meta Brand Partner Ads with separate fee, (5) exclusivity — categories I cannot post for, duration in days, geographic scope, (6) disclosure compliance — explicit ASCI 2024 line that 'creator will tag Paid Partnership and add #ad in first 3 lines, with clear verbal disclosure if applicable', (7) payment terms — 50% advance on signing, 50% within 15 days of go-live, late-payment 1.5% per month, GST extra at 18%, (8) cancellation and IP — script ownership, raw footage delivery yes/no, kill fee 50% if cancelled post-shoot, (9) dispute resolution — Indian arbitration seat. Output the full template ready to copy-paste, plus a Hinglish cover note.

prompts.title: Content Rights and Whitelisting Clauses
prompts.prompt: Act as a media-rights lawyer who has reviewed 200+ creator-brand contracts in India. Walk me through the content-rights clauses I must understand and negotiate before signing a brand reel deal. Output: (1) the difference between organic-use rights (brand can re-share to their handle), paid-boost rights (brand boosts my reel as ad on their account), whitelisting / Meta Brand Partner Ads (brand runs ads from MY handle to lookalike audiences, my followers see 'Sponsored' under my name) — and the rate uplift for each (typically 30%, 60-100%, 100-200% of base reel fee), (2) duration — 30 / 90 / 180 days / perpetual, with clear sunset behaviour, (3) territory — India only, India + GCC NRI, global, (4) editing rights — can the brand re-edit my reel into a 6-sec ad cutdown? add their voice-over? change the music? these need explicit yes/no, (5) renewal mechanic — brand pays X% to extend usage by another 90 days, (6) take-down right — I can request removal if there's a brand reputation issue (food adulteration scandal, pyramid scheme exposure), (7) personality rights — my face, voice, name, signature catchphrase cannot be used outside the agreed reel, (8) a clause I should always add: ASCI compliance is a joint responsibility, brand cannot remove my disclosure in their re-edit. Provide 5 sample clause texts I can paste into any contract.

prompts.title: Collab Strategy with Other Creators
prompts.prompt: You are a creator-collaboration matchmaker who has paired Indian Reels creators for collab posts using Instagram's Collab feature, growing both pages by 20-50K each in single drops. Design me a collab strategy for the next 90 days. Output: (1) the partner-fit framework — same niche or adjacent (food + travel, fashion + beauty), audience overlap target 15-30% (too low = no transfer, too high = no new reach), follower count within 0.5x-2x of mine, posting cadence aligned, brand-safety values aligned, (2) a 5-step outreach DM, (3) the collab-format menu — split-screen reaction reel, duet-style point-counterpoint, swap-day where we shoot on each other's themes, joint-product giveaway, joint live, joint-festival lookbook, (4) the technical Instagram Collab feature — how to invite a co-author so the reel posts to BOTH grids and shares ALL metrics, the rules (only 1 co-author until late-2025, then 3 allowed), (5) the equity split — both creators write captions in their voice, both reply to comments, both stories tease, (6) the post-collab follow-up — DM the new followers a welcome message via story-link sticker, recap reel a week later cementing the pair as a recurring duo, (7) collab-page expansion — building a third joint handle for sustained co-content. Provide a target list of 10 partner archetypes for my niche [NICHE].

prompts.title: Creator-Page Handover for Collab Post
prompts.prompt: Act as a creator-operations consultant. The 'creator-page handover' is when another creator briefly takes over my Reel or my page for a day — high-trust collab that can move 30-100K followers in either direction. Build me a handover SOP. Output: (1) the formats — guest-host my reel (they appear on my page in my style), I guest-host theirs, full 24-hour story takeover with one collab reel pinned, week-long reel series across both pages, (2) the safety pre-flight — credential boundaries (never share my password, use Meta Business Suite delegation), content veto (I retain final cut), brand-deal disclosure (if either of us has live brand exclusivity), (3) the brief I send the guest creator — my voice, three forbidden topics, dress code, language preference, the closing CTA they must say, (4) the cross-promo loop — 5 stories from each side over the 24-48 hour window, pinned reel for 7 days, joint live during peak 9-11pm IST slot, (5) the post-event audit — net followers gained, comment-quality, DM volume, save-rate, brand inbound increase, (6) the friendship economics — never demand reciprocity in the first round, build relational capital, the second round will be more equal. Provide a worked example of two creators in [NICHE] doing a 7-day handover series and the projected outcomes.

prompts.title: Niche-Pivot Plan Without Losing Followers
prompts.prompt: You are a creator-strategy consultant who has guided pivots like comedy-to-mental-health, fashion-to-finance, food-to-travel, fitness-to-mom-life. The hard truth: most pivots cost 20-40% of followers in the first quarter but the right pivot can 3-5x revenue within 12 months. Design me a niche-pivot plan from [CURRENT NICHE] to [TARGET NICHE]. Output: (1) the audit — how much of my current audience secretly wants the new content (poll via stories, scan DMs, look at saves on adjacent reels), (2) the 'bridge phase' — 8 weeks where I post 70% old niche, 30% new niche, slowly shifting to 30/70, (3) the on-platform announcement — a vulnerable, story-led reel explaining why I'm pivoting, what stays, what changes, (4) the bio rewrite — new tagline, new pinned reels, new highlight covers, new Linktree, (5) the content calendar for the first 90 days post-pivot with weekly themes, (6) the brand-deal communication — informing existing brand partners, releasing exclusivity, repositioning rate-card to new category multipliers, (7) the metric guardrails — if follower drop exceeds 25% in first 60 days, slow the pivot speed; if engagement on new niche outperforms old by 40%+, accelerate, (8) Indian-context examples of three creators who pivoted well and three who pivoted poorly (described archetypally), with the lessons. End with a one-page pivot manifesto template.

prompts.title: Giveaway Mechanics that Don't Hurt Quality Followers
prompts.prompt: Act as a community-growth tactician. Indian creators love giveaways but most attract junk followers who unfollow within a week, hurting reach. Design me a high-quality giveaway mechanic. Output: (1) the prize design — niche-specific premium item over generic iPhone (e.g. for a fashion creator: a 25K rupee saree from a Banarasi weaver, for a fitness creator: 6-month gym membership at a known chain, for a food creator: dinner-for-two at a 5-star), (2) entry rules that filter for niche-fit followers — 'follow me, save this reel, comment your favourite saree colour, tag one friend who would wear this' — no cross-following 5 random pages, no tag-five-friends spam, (3) the disclosure — 'Giveaway closes [date], winner picked via random draw on live, sponsored prize provided by @brand, not affiliated with Meta', meeting both ASCI and Meta's promotional-content rules, (4) the brand-funded model — partnering with 1-3 brands who fund the prize in exchange for tagging and a sponsored reel, monetising the giveaway itself for 50K-3L per drop, (5) the live-draw reel — 60-second compilation of the random pick, winner reveal, congratulatory DM, (6) the post-giveaway nurture — 30-day welcome series of stories so the new followers don't immediately churn, (7) frequency — max once a quarter, never monthly. Provide a worked giveaway plan for a 70K-follower regional creator.

prompts.title: Event-Coverage Plan for IIFA, Lakme Fashion Week, Filmfare
prompts.prompt: You are a Reels event-coverage producer. Indian creators get invited to IIFA, Lakme Fashion Week, Filmfare, Femina, Vogue, Filmfare South, Mirchi Awards, IPL closing, Sunburn, Magnetic Fields, Sula Fest, GIFLIF — and most leave with chaotic phone footage they can't edit into anything cohesive. Build me an event-coverage plan I can deploy any time I get a media pass. Output: (1) the pre-event prep — 1-week-out 'I'm going to X' anticipation reel, a follower poll asking what they want me to capture, an outfit reveal teaser, (2) the on-ground shot list — red-carpet establish wide, my arrival POV, 3 micro-interviews with attendees in 10 seconds each, 1 backstage moment, 1 candid celebrity sighting, 1 audience-mood pan, 1 stage performance clip (only 5 sec for copyright safety), 1 outfit close-up, 1 event-food shot, 1 night-end exhausted-but-happy selfie reel, (3) the daily upload rhythm — 1 reel within 3 hours of red carpet, 3 stories every 2 hours, recap reel at 11pm same night, (4) the post-event compilation — a 60-second BTS reel + a fashion-roundup reel + a 'who wore what' carousel within 48 hours, (5) the brand collab integration — wearing a sponsoring brand on the carpet with disclosure, tagging the event-sponsor in the recap, (6) safety and credential etiquette to keep getting invited. Provide a checklist printable for shoot day.

prompts.title: Festival Content Calendar for Indian Creators
prompts.prompt: Act as a content-calendar planner specialising in the Indian festival cycle, regional and national. Build me a 12-month festival content calendar for my niche [NICHE] with reel ideas for each festival. Cover: Lohri / Pongal / Bihu / Makar Sankranti (Jan), Republic Day, Saraswati Puja, Valentine's, Maha Shivratri, Holi (Mar), Ugadi / Gudi Padwa / Vishu / Bengali New Year (Apr), Eid-ul-Fitr, Buddha Purnima, Mother's Day, Father's Day, Eid-ul-Adha, Onam (Aug-Sep), Ganesh Chaturthi, Janmashtami, Independence Day, Raksha Bandhan, Navratri (9 nights, 9 colours, 9 reel ideas), Dussehra, Karwa Chauth, Diwali (5-day arc with reel per day), Bhai Dooj, Children's Day, Christmas, New Year. For each festival give: (1) reel concept tailored to my niche, (2) the 7-day pre-festival hashtag push, (3) trending audio archetype for that festival in 2026, (4) brand-deal opportunity (which brands buy festival-themed reels — sweets brands, jewellery, ethnic-wear, gifting platforms, dry-fruit brands), (5) regional creator note — Onam-heavy Kerala creators, Bihu-heavy Assam, Pongal-heavy Tamil Nadu, Durga Puja-heavy Bengal — leverage local-language reels for that fortnight. End with a printable 12-month wall-poster calendar mapping festivals to my publishing slots.

prompts.title: Creator Wedding-Album Reel Strategy
prompts.prompt: You are a creator-economy strategist. The Indian creator wedding is a 6-12 month content arc that can drive a follower jump of 100K-1M and brand deals worth 30L-3Cr (lehenga, jewellery, venue, photographer, makeup, mehendi, choreographer, gifting). Build me a wedding-album reel strategy. Cover: (1) the 12-month timeline starting from engagement reveal — proposal reel, ring close-up, save-the-date video, pre-wedding shoot drop, mehendi day, haldi, sangeet rehearsal reel, choreo behind-the-scenes, the actual sangeet performance reel, wedding day morning GRWM, varmala moment, vidaai edit, reception, honeymoon vlog, post-wedding nameplate reveal, (2) the brand-stack negotiation — bundling all wedding vendors as paid partners with disclosure on each, packaging as a 30-event brand portfolio rather than one-offs, (3) the disclosure choreography — every paid partner gets 'Paid partnership' tag, the first 3 reels MUST clearly disclose to set audience expectations and avoid ASCI complaint cascade (high-profile creator weddings draw scrutiny), (4) the 'human moments' rule — for every 3 sponsored reels, 1 unsponsored emotional reel (mum hugging me, dad's speech) so the audience doesn't feel the wedding is one long ad, (5) the post-wedding repurpose — 90-day 'wedding behind the scenes' carousel series, anniversary reel a year later, (6) the pre-nup content rights with my partner if they're not a creator. Provide a sample 6-month plan for a 200K creator marrying in November.

prompts.title: Regional Dub Strategy for Pan-India Reach
prompts.prompt: Act as a multilingual content strategist. Indian Reels in 2026 reward language-native content: a Hindi reel rarely cracks Tamil Nadu, a Tamil reel rarely cracks UP. Top creators now publish dubbed regional versions to multiply reach 3-5x. Design me a regional-dub strategy for my flagship reels. Output: (1) the source-language master reel — script, voice-over, on-screen text in my native language, (2) the target-language priority list based on my niche — fashion creators dub to Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Malayalam, Bengali, Marathi, Punjabi; food creators to language of the dish's region first, (3) the dub-execution options — self-dub if I'm bilingual, friend-creator dub-swap (you dub mine, I dub yours), AI dub via tools like ElevenLabs / HeyGen / Sieve / Murf AI / Krutrim — and the quality threshold (AI Hindi/English passable, AI Bengali/Tamil still detectably off in 2026, must verify with a native speaker), (4) the publishing cadence — original on day 1, regional versions on day 3, 5, 7 across separate handles or as 'language version' reels on the same handle, (5) the on-screen-text translation — must be re-typed in the regional script, not just romanised, (6) the cultural localisation — references, food names, festival callouts adapted (a Hindi 'maa ke haath ka khaana' becomes 'amma kai saapadu' in Tamil), (7) brand-deal pitch — bundle regional dubs as a +50% deliverable. Provide 3 worked dub plans across niches.

prompts.title: NRI Audience Targeting Strategy
prompts.prompt: You are a creator strategist who specialises in the NRI Indian diaspora market — UAE, US, UK, Canada, Australia, Singapore — collectively a 25M+ audience with disposable income who will pay 2-5x more for Indian-creator products and 1.5-3x premium on brand deals because brands use Indian creators to reach NRI consumers. Build me an NRI-targeting reel strategy for my niche [NICHE]. Output: (1) content themes that resonate with NRIs — nostalgia (90s Bollywood, school-tiffin food, Doordarshan), homesick festivals abroad (Diwali in Toronto, Onam in Dubai, Holi in London), India-vs-abroad comparisons, settling-back-in-India guides, dual-passport visa journeys, sending-gifts-to-India hacks, (2) the posting time — 7-9pm GMT for UK/EU, 6-9pm EST for US East, 7-9pm GST for UAE — which means scheduling 2 daily slots not just IST, (3) the language mix — English-Hindi 60-40 ratio works best as it includes NRI kids who don't read Devanagari, (4) the brand-deal angle — pitch NRI-targeting brands (DesiVida, IndianGiftsPortal, ShaadiMatch, IndusInd NRI banking, Air India, money-transfer apps Wise / Remitly / Western Union, Indian-grocery delivery in US/UK like Quicklly, Patel Brothers), (5) the geo-tag and hashtag mix — #nriindians #desiabroad #indianintheus #indianincanada, (6) collaboration with NRI-based creators for trans-Atlantic reach swaps. Provide 10 NRI-targeted reel concepts.

prompts.title: Paid-Promo Sponsored Reel Disclosure (ASCI 2024)
prompts.prompt: Act as an ASCI-compliance consultant. The Advertising Standards Council of India 2024 guidelines (with 2025 updates) specifically govern influencer reels and have escalated enforcement, with multiple Indian creators receiving public notices in 2024-25 including some 1M+ creators. Build me a sponsored-reel disclosure SOP. Cover: (1) the four-channel disclosure — (a) on-screen visible text 'Paid Partnership' for the first 3 seconds AND any time the brand is shown, (b) spoken verbal disclosure within first 5 seconds in the language of the reel, (c) caption first line must contain '#ad' or '#sponsored' or 'Paid partnership with @brand' (Instagram's native Paid Partnership tag does NOT replace this), (d) Meta's Branded Content tag enabled, (2) the gift / barter rule — even unpaid PR products worth above 1 received gift threshold need disclosure, (3) the 'material connection' rule — if I have any equity, affiliate fee, free service, family relationship with the brand, all must be disclosed, (4) financial / health / cosmetic / edtech reels have stricter rules — must add 'results may vary', cite source for stats, no medical claims without qualified-doctor on screen, (5) the deletion-and-edit risk — if I edit a reel post-publication to remove a disclosure, ASCI treats it as wilful violation, (6) the penalty cascade — ASCI complaint, brand contract termination, potential CCPA notice, in extreme cases account-restriction. Provide a one-page disclosure cheat-sheet for me to pin near my desk.

prompts.title: Hook Bank for Fashion Niche
prompts.prompt: You are a fashion-Reels writer who has crafted hooks for top Indian fashion creators across saree, indo-western fusion, streetwear, modest fashion, plus-size, petite, men's fashion. Build me a 40-hook bank specifically for the fashion niche. Hooks must be in Hinglish or my regional language [LANGUAGE], delivered in under 3 seconds, optimised to make a scrolling thumb stop. Cover hook archetypes: (1) price-shock — 'Yeh saree sirf 800 ki', (2) styling-secret — 'Yeh dupatta-trick mummy ne sikhayi', (3) body-confidence — 'Plus size ke liye yeh cut sabse flattering', (4) trend-prediction — '2026 mein yeh outfits bahut chalengi', (5) celeb-dupe — 'Alia ke lehenga ka 5000 wala dupe', (6) wedding-utility — 'Sister ki shaadi mein sasta but stunning', (7) anti-aunty — 'Aunties bolengi galat hai but try karke dekho', (8) festive-unlock — 'Karwa Chauth ke 7 days, 7 looks', (9) regional-pride — 'Banarasi vs Kanjivaram vs Patola', (10) brand-spotlight — 'Yeh affordable brand discover kiya'. For each hook give: spoken line, on-screen overlay, opening visual (close-up of fabric, mirror twirl, hanger reveal), recommended audio archetype, and the niche-sub-segment it serves. Add 5 hooks I should retire because they're now overused on Indian fashion Reels.

prompts.title: Hook Bank for Fitness Niche
prompts.prompt: Act as a fitness-Reels copywriter. Indian fitness creators face a saturated feed (gym selfies, generic workouts) and ASCI scrutiny on weight-loss claims. Build me a 30-hook bank that stands out without making medical promises. Hooks must be in Hinglish or my chosen language. Cover archetypes: (1) myth-bust — 'Roti khane se motapa nahi hota — yeh sach hai', (2) at-home-no-equipment — '0 rupee gym, 100% results', (3) PCOS / thyroid / desi-women body realities — 'PCOS hai? Yeh 5 exercises change kar denge', (4) auntie-friendly — '40+ ke baad yeh start karo', (5) nutrition-on-budget — '100 rupee mein 100g protein', (6) form-fix — 'Squat galat kar rahe ho — 90% log yeh galti karte hain', (7) wedding-fitness — 'Shaadi se 90 din pehle yeh routine', (8) male-mental-health-and-fitness — 'Bro, gym depression bhi theek karta hai', (9) pre-workout-meal — 'Khaali pet workout matt karo', (10) post-shaadi-reset — 'Honeymoon ke baad fitness wapas kaise laaye'. For each hook: spoken line, opening visual, on-screen text, audio mood, ASCI-safe phrasing (no 'guaranteed weight loss', no 'cure for diabetes'), the affiliate or product it pairs with (whey protein, fitness app subscription, gym wear). Provide 10 fully-shot reel scripts using these hooks.

prompts.title: Hook Bank for Food Niche
prompts.prompt: You are a food-Reels writer who has scripted hooks for top Indian food creators in regional cuisine, street food, fusion, healthy desi, baking, restaurant reviews. Build me a 35-hook bank for the food niche. Hooks must trigger appetite within 2 seconds (sizzle, steam, cheese-pull) and a verbal hook. Cover archetypes: (1) regional-deep-cut — 'Pune ka misal pav jo sirf locals jaante hain', (2) under-100-rupee — 'Sirf 80 rupee mein restaurant-style biryani', (3) one-ingredient-secret — 'Dal ka taste 10x kaisa karein? Yeh ek cheez', (4) street-food-vs-home — 'Pani puri ghar pe banayi, taste 1:1', (5) granny-recipe — 'Ammama ki secret kuzhambu — kabhi share nahi hui thi', (6) restaurant-roast — 'Mumbai ka yeh restaurant overrated hai — proof yeh hai', (7) festival-special — 'Diwali ki kaju katli — burfi se zyada easy', (8) healthy-makeover — 'Maggie ka healthy version 5 minute mein', (9) bachelor-friendly — 'Hostel mein 1-pan dinner', (10) regional creators — 'Bengali aloo posto', 'Andhra gongura pachadi', 'Goan vindaloo', 'Kashmiri rogan josh'. For each hook: spoken line in Hinglish or regional, the hero visual (top-down vs hand-held vs eat-shot), audio cue (sizzle, dhol drop, slow-mo crunch), call-to-action ('Save this recipe', 'Tag a foodie'), brand-deal angle (masala brand, ghee, cookware). Add 5 hooks that are now cringe.

prompts.title: Hook Bank for Motivation Niche
prompts.prompt: Act as a motivational-Reels writer. The Indian motivation niche is saturated with Gita-quotes-on-black-background and gym-bro alpha content. Build me a 30-hook bank that stands apart with emotional honesty and practical edge. Hooks should be in Hindi/Hinglish/regional, delivered in 3 seconds, paired with strong visual or face-to-camera direct address. Cover archetypes: (1) reality-check — 'Tu lag raha hai busy, lekin actually time waste kar raha hai', (2) middle-class-grind — 'Mummy-papa ne 30 saal kaam kiya, ab teri baari', (3) anti-hustle-culture — 'Burnout glamorous nahi hai', (4) failure-honesty — 'Maine 4 startup band kiye, yeh seekha', (5) loneliness-of-success — 'Jab pehli baar 1 lakh kamaye, kisi ko bata nahi paya', (6) Indian-dad-archetype — 'Papa kabhi hug nahi karte the, lekin yeh karte the', (7) small-town-dream — 'Aurangabad se Bombay tak ka safar', (8) student-burnout — 'JEE ki rat 11 baje yeh sochna ki rok', (9) women-specific — 'Shaadi ke baad bhi career — yeh kaise', (10) tactical-not-vague — 'Goal achieve karne ka 4-step framework, vague nahi'. For each hook: spoken line, opening visual (face-to-camera, walking shot, journal close-up), background audio (slow piano, lo-fi, soft Bollywood acoustic), CTA (save, share, DM 'YES'), warning against fake-guru tropes. Add a 'do not say' list — hollow phrases like 'just believe in yourself' that Indian Gen Z viewers now mock.

prompts.title: Hook Bank for Education Niche
prompts.prompt: You are an education-Reels writer for Indian creators in finance, English-fluency, UPSC, productivity, parenting, mental-health, STEM, career-coaching. Build me a 30-hook bank engineered for save-and-share rather than just like. Hooks must promise concrete value within 3 seconds. Cover archetypes: (1) money-mistake — '90% Indians SIP galat tarike se start karte hain', (2) language-shame-free — 'Yeh English mistakes interview mein bahut bachenge', (3) UPSC-tactical — 'Mains essay mein top 1% likhne ka structure', (4) career-pivot — 'Engineer se UX designer kaise bani — 6 mahine ka roadmap', (5) parenting-no-judgement — 'Toddler tantrum handle karne ka science-backed tareeka', (6) mental-health-de-stigma — 'Therapy India mein affordable kaise', (7) STEM-curiosity — 'Black hole ka Hindi mein simple explanation', (8) productivity-anti-fluff — 'Time-block method jo actually kaam karta hai, Pomodoro nahi', (9) freelance-economics — 'Freelancer ka first 50K monthly milestone', (10) Indian-tax-101 — 'New regime vs old regime — kis ke liye kya'. For each hook: spoken line, on-screen number/stat overlay, source-citation in caption (mandatory for educational content to avoid misinformation flag), the lead-magnet hook ('Comment WORKBOOK for free PDF'), the brand-deal angle (edtech app, fintech, books, courses). Provide 10 worked scripts with full slide-deck reel format.

prompts.title: Hook Bank for Comedy Niche
prompts.prompt: Act as a stand-up writer who scripts comedy reels for Indian creators in observational, character, sketch, parody, regional comedy. Build me a 30-hook bank where the hook itself sets up the laugh. Hooks should be in Hindi/Hinglish/regional and play on relatable Indian universals without punching down. Cover archetypes: (1) WhatsApp-uncle — 'Forwarded message uncle ka aaj ka theory', (2) Indian-mum impression — 'Beta, beta, beta — fir bhi meri sunta nahi', (3) Bangalore-traffic — 'Silk Board mein meri zindagi ki seekh', (4) Mumbai-local — 'Andheri-Churchgate ke 7 character', (5) Delhi-aunty — 'Delhi shaadi mein har aunty puchhti hai yeh sawaal', (6) sasural — 'Pehli Karwa Chauth, pehla pakwan, pehla blunder', (7) corporate-roast — 'Manager ne yeh kaha, samajh kya gaya', (8) school-nostalgia — '90s school PT teacher universe', (9) regional pride — 'Tamil mum vs Punjabi mum vs Bengali mum', (10) Gen-Z vs Millennial — 'Cousin ka Insta bio kya bolta hai'. For each hook: spoken setup line, character voice/accent, costume / prop, escalation beats, punchline, the 'first comment to seed', cultural-sensitivity guard rails (no caste, religion, regional-supremacy jokes — the 2024 takedown waves taught creators to stay safe). Add 5 cringe hooks now retired in 2026.

prompts.title: Regional Creator Playbook — Kannada / Tamil / Telugu / Malayalam
prompts.prompt: You are a South-Indian creator-economy strategist. Reels in Kannada / Tamil / Telugu / Malayalam often have higher engagement rates than Hindi but lower brand-deal rates per follower. Build me a playbook to flip that economic disadvantage. Output: (1) the audience-native reel formats — Sandalwood / Kollywood / Tollywood / Mollywood movie reactions, regional-festival reels (Onam, Pongal, Ugadi, Vishu), local-food deep dives, dialect comedy, regional mythology (Yakshagana, Kathakali references), (2) the hashtag stack — #kannadareels #tamilreels #telugureels #malayalamreels plus city-specific #bengalurudiaries #chennaiculture #hyderabadi #kochilife, (3) the cross-language bridge — subtitling regional reels in English to capture pan-India and NRI viewers without losing native authenticity, (4) the brand-deal economics — pitching D2C brands targeting South India (ID Fresh, iD Idli, Sangeetha sarees, Joyalukkas, Malabar Gold, Tanishq South-India campaigns), positioning regional creators with REAL state-level reach data (not just follower count), demanding language-multiplier in negotiations (regional creators command 1.2-1.5x for South-India-focused campaigns), (5) the audio strategy — regional-language trending sounds vs pan-India Bollywood — ratios per niche, (6) the cross-state collab map — Kannada creator + Tamil creator joint reels for the South-India belt. Provide 10 reel concepts and a brand-pitch DM in English plus my regional language.

prompts.title: Regional Creator Playbook — Bengali / Marathi / Punjabi / Gujarati
prompts.prompt: Act as a regional creator strategist for the Bengali / Marathi / Punjabi / Gujarati creator scenes. Each has distinct cultural rhythms — Bengali creators thrive on Durga Puja and intellectualism, Marathi on Ganesh Chaturthi and Pune-Mumbai duality, Punjabi on music and lifestyle bling, Gujarati on entrepreneurship and Navratri. Build me a region-specific reel playbook for [REGION I PICK]. Output: (1) the cultural-festival anchor calendar — for Bengali: Durga Puja 5-day arc, Saraswati Puja, Poila Boishakh; for Marathi: Ganesh Chaturthi 11 days, Gudi Padwa, Diwali padwa; for Punjabi: Lohri, Baisakhi, Karwa Chauth, wedding season; for Gujarati: Navratri 9 nights, Diwali, Uttarayan, (2) the audio archetypes — Bengali Rabindrasangeet remixes and adda dialogues, Marathi powada and Bollywood-Marathi crossover, Punjabi Diljit / AP Dhillon / Karan Aujla beats, Gujarati garba and dandiya, (3) niche-fit reel formats — Bengali food reels on machher jhol and rosogolla, Marathi misal pav and modak, Punjabi sarso da saag and butter chicken, Gujarati dhokla and thepla, (4) brand-deal map — regional D2C brands (Bengali: Bangalonatok, Patrali; Marathi: Suhana masale; Punjabi: Verka, KKB; Gujarati: Haldiram's, Patanjali, Bombay Shaving), (5) language-mix advice — when to go fully regional vs hybrid with Hindi/English subtitles, (6) creator-collab pairings within the region. Provide 10 reel concepts and a regional brand-pitch DM template.

prompts.title: Reels Analytics Audit and Weekly Review
prompts.prompt: You are a data analyst for Indian Reels creators. Build me a weekly analytics audit I can run every Sunday in 30 minutes. Output: (1) the metrics that matter in 2026 — Instagram has shifted weight to reach, watch-time, send-rate (DM share), saves, follows-from-reel, profile-visits, comments-from-reach %, while likes are now decorative, (2) the dashboard template (Notion or Sheets) with columns — reel, niche, hook archetype, audio type, length, paid/organic/hybrid, reach, plays, average-watch-time, completion %, save:like, share:like, follow-conversion, total cost to produce, (3) the per-week analysis routine — top 3 reels and bottom 3 reels, what the top 3 had in common (audio? hook? length? CTA?), the 'kill list' of formats to retire, the 'amplify list' of formats to repeat, (4) the 90-day cohort view — has my median reach grown? has my follower-quality (story-views as % of followers) held above 5%? are saves growing faster than likes? (5) the brand-deal insight — which reels did brand managers screenshot from my insights tab during pitch, (6) the alert thresholds — if average reach drops 30%+ for 2 consecutive weeks, run a content-fatigue audit; if shares drop, hooks are weak; if saves drop, value-density is weak. Provide a sample audit for a 50K creator showing real decisions and a one-page dashboard layout.

prompts.title: From Brand-Deal Income to Own-Product Launch
prompts.prompt: Act as a creator-economy business consultant. Brand deals are good income but capped — own-product income is the real wealth lever. Build me a roadmap to launch my own product from my creator handle in [NICHE] with my current audience size [SIZE]. Output: (1) the product-fit menu by niche — fashion creator: capsule clothing drop / hand-painted dupattas, fitness: workout PDF or 6-week program, food: recipe ebook or masala blend, motivation: paid community on Stan/Beacons, education: cohort course on Zoom / GrowthSchool, lifestyle: physical journal, comedy: merch, (2) the validation layer — pre-sell to 30 audience members at 50% off via DM before manufacturing or finalising, (3) the launch-week reel sequence — 5 reels over 7 days teasing, behind-the-scenes, founder-story, customer-quote, scarcity-deadline reel, (4) the funnel — Reels to bio link to product page on Stan / Razorpay storefront / Shopify / Kajabi, with abandoned-cart DM recovery, (5) the unit economics — example for a 599-rupee digital product, 1000 sales = 6L revenue, 80% margin = 4.8L profit, more than most brand deals at 50K-follower scale, (6) the GST and MSME registration practicalities — sole prop GST registration above 20L turnover, FSSAI for any food product, ASCI applies to my own-product reels too, (7) the recurring-launch cadence — quarterly drops, customer-cohort marketing, loyalty discount. Provide a 90-day launch plan.

prompts.title: Creator Burnout Prevention and Sustainable Cadence
prompts.prompt: You are a creator-wellness coach who has worked with 200+ Indian creators across the burnout-comeback spectrum. The reel-a-day grind that built many Indian creators in 2020-23 is killing them in 2026, with public mental-health breakdowns and 6-12 month hiatuses becoming common. Design me a sustainable cadence and burnout-prevention system. Output: (1) the cadence-by-niche realism — fashion 4-5 reels/week, fitness 3-4, food 4-5, motivation 3, comedy 3-4, education 3, regional 3-4 — and why daily-posting is no longer required for algorithmic favour in 2026, (2) the batching system — 1 shoot day for 7 reels, 1 edit day, 5 publish days, 1 rest day, 1 strategy day, (3) the camera-off rule — 1 day a week with phone in another room, no scrolling, no posting, (4) the comment-overload defence — turning off comment notifications, replying in scheduled 30-minute blocks, hiring a part-time community-manager at 8K-15K/month from a 50K-follower stage, (5) the content-fatigue early-warning signals — dread before shoots, performative crying for views, comparing self to bigger creators daily, drop in shower / sleep / eating regularity, (6) the hiatus protocol — how to take a planned 14-30 day break with audience permission instead of an unplanned ghost, story-pinned message, return-reel script, (7) the long-game money math — sustainable creators at 5 reels/week for 5 years out-earn burnout creators at 2 reels/day for 18 months. Provide a 30-day reset plan.
